name: El Rodenal Trail
name_original: Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular trailrunning por el Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo
description: Jog the moderate El Rodenal Trail, a 7.9-mile route in Alto Tajo Nature Park, featuring the Barranco de la Hoz Viewpoint.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Corduente are set within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, characterized by diverse and striking natural features. The region features the Barranco de la Hoz, with its red sandstone formations and the Gallo River, alongside extensive pine and oak forests. Fluvial gorges and canyons shaped by rivers create varied terrain, offering a mix of paths from relatively easy tracks to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Corduente The most popular jogging route is Tajo Natural Trail Loop, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers scenic views within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route explores historical landscapes and forest paths. Local runners also love the El Rodenal Trail, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Corduente is defined by red sandstone formations, extensive pine forests, and fluvial gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Corduente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Corduente?
A: Corduente offers a selection of 8 dedicated jogging routes, providing a variety of options for exploring the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Corduente?
A: While most routes in Corduente are classified as moderate or difficult, there are no routes specifically rated as 'easy' in this guide. However, the area offers varied terrain, and some moderate paths through pine forests might be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience. Always check the route details for elevation and distance before you go.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Corduente area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Corduente are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032338247/tajo-natural-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo Natural Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031411653/camino-del-cid-molina-de-aragon-and-castilnuevo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while jogging around Corduente?
A: Jogging around Corduente offers a diverse landscape within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter magnificent red sandstone formations in the Barranco de la Hoz, extensive pine and oak forests, and fluvial gorges carved by rivers like the Gallo. The terrain varies from relatively flat tracks to more challenging ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights along the running trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz&lt;/a&gt;, built into a rock face at the bottom of Barranco de la Hoz, which can be a destination point for a run. The area also features historic castles like the Castillo de Santiuste and is part of the Camino del Cid, offering glimpses into historical landscapes.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Corduente suitable for families?
A: While the guide does not specifically list 'easy' routes, some of the moderate paths through pine forests might be suitable for active families. However, many routes involve varied terrain, including steep sections. It's recommended to check the specific route details, including distance and elevation, to determine suitability for your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Corduente?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park, where Corduente is located, generally allows dogs on trails, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check local regulations or specific trail signage before heading out with your pet.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing the running routes?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes in natural parks typically have designated parking areas near popular access points or villages. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route description on komoot for details on starting points and potential parking facilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Corduente?
A: The best time for running in Corduente is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are preferable. Winters can be cold, but the trails remain accessible, offering a different, serene experience.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural landmarks to look out for?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramas of the red sandstone gorge and the Gallo River. You might also encounter the prominent El Huso Monolith, a 40-meter-high conglomerate formation, adding a unique geological point of interest to the landscape.

Q: What makes the terrain around Corduente unique for trail running?
A: The terrain around Corduente is unique due to its location within the UNESCO Global Geopark of Molina and Alto Tajo. It features dramatic red sandstone formations, deep fluvial gorges, and a mix of pine and oak forests. This diverse environment provides a challenging and visually stunning experience for trail runners, with varied paths from forest tracks to rocky ascents.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Yes, for experienced trail runners looking for a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031411653/camino-del-cid-molina-de-aragon-and-castilnuevo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: Molina de Aragón and Castilnuevo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 7.9 miles (12.7 km), or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032344746/el-rodenal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rodenal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also 7.9 miles (12.7 km), offer significant distances and moderate difficulty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032338247/tajo-natural-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo Natural Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult and covers 5.4 miles (8.8 km) with notable elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about the trails in Corduente?
A: Runners frequently praise the stunning natural beauty and varied terrain of Corduente. The dramatic red sandstone formations of Barranco de la Hoz, the peaceful pine forests, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents and descents are often highlighted as favorite aspects of the running experience in this region.
tips: Viewpoint with aerial views of a geological formation caused over time by the erosion of the Gallo River. True sculptures and reddish rock formations have been formed in a fluvial canyon, leaving behind some of the most impressive gorges in this Natural Park.

Hospedería Virgen de la Hoz is a charming rural accommodation. This place offers a peaceful and natural setting, ideal for relaxing and enjoying outdoor activities such as hiking, cycling and bird watching.
The inn has cosy and well-equipped rooms, a restaurant serving delicious à la carte dishes, and a terrace for relaxing. In addition, guests can enjoy Wi-Fi in public areas.

faqs.question: Where does the El Rodenal Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The El Rodenal Trail is a circular route that starts and ends near the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for trails near popular landmarks like sanctuaries to have designated parking areas nearby. We recommend checking local maps or community forums for the most precise parking information before your visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the El Rodenal Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is approximately 12.7 kilometers (7.9 miles) long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 33 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the El Rodenal Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The El Rodenal Trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it has a manageable elevation gain of about 230 meters, the terrain can include technical features like rock gardens and 'skinny' sections, especially if it aligns with the 'Rodenal Multi Trail' description. Beginners with some prior trail running experience might find it challenging but rewarding, while absolute novices might prefer an easier route first.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Rodenal Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain. Based on descriptions of similar 'Rodenal' trails, you can expect a mix of singletrack, potentially with technical sections, rock gardens, and possibly 'red stone' landscapes. The route includes both ascents and descents, offering a dynamic jogging experience.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the El Rodenal Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter two notable highlights. You can enjoy views from the Barranco de la Hoz Viewpoint and visit the historic Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz. The trail is also situated within the beautiful natural landscapes of the Alto Tajo Nature Park.

faqs.question: Is the El Rodenal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are permitted on the El Rodenal Trail. However, it's important to keep them on a leash at all times and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment and respect other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the El Rodenal Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in a natural park, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and beautiful foliage. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter jogging is possible, but be prepared for potentially colder temperatures and check for any specific conditions or closures.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the El Rodenal Trail or Alto Tajo Nature Park?
faqs.answer: The El Rodenal Trail is located within the Alto Tajo Nature Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or jogging on trails within Spanish natural parks. However, it's always a good idea to check the official website for Alto Tajo Nature Park before your visit for any updated regulations or specific requirements, especially concerning activities like camping or large group events.

faqs.question: Does the El Rodenal Trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route you'll be jogging incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 50% of the route follows the designated 'El Rodenal' trail, while 26% is part of 'Las Tres Taifas', and a smaller portion (3%) uses the 'Camino del Cementerio'.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in a natural setting. While specific amenities directly at the trailhead are not detailed, the presence of the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz suggests there might be some basic facilities or small establishments in nearby villages. For more extensive options like cafes, restaurants, or accommodation, you would likely need to visit larger towns in the vicinity of the Alto Tajo Nature Park.

faqs.question: Is the El Rodenal Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Rodenal Trail is designed as a circular route, meaning you will start and finish at the same point, near the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz
